commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=42cd9015500b055d946dc4832237585d0cd52a5a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Codex app-server provider-aware approval reviewer gate and regression tests so local/custom providers use user approvals instead of Guardian/auto_review across run attempts, side questions, and bound conversations.

PR surface: Source +66, Tests +370. Total +436 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main maps tools.exec.mode=auto to auto_review and forwards appServer.approvalsReviewer through run-attempt, side-question, and conversation-binding paths without a local/custom provider gate. I did not run a live local-model app-server.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the PR is maintainer-protected and changes security-sensitive approval routing, with remaining requirements/docs behavior that needs owner acceptance.

target.title: fix(codex): avoid guardian review for local models
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Prevents Codex app-server Guardian / `auto_review` from being used as the approval reviewer when the active model provider is local or custom, such as LM Studio.
- Keeps `tools.exec.mode=auto` guarded for local/custom providers by preserving prompting approvals and sandboxing while routing the review to a human.
- Applies the same provider-aware reviewer policy to normal Codex app-server runs, bound conversation turns, and side-question forks.

Why does this matter now?

- `tools.exec.mode=auto` forced Codex Guardian before checking the selected model provider. A local model routed through the Codex harness could therefore be treated as the safety reviewer.

What is the intended outcome?

- Codex-native and OpenAI-backed Codex app-server runs can keep model-backed approval review.
- Local/custom providers fall back to `approvalsReviewer: "user"` while retaining `approvalPolicy: "on-request"` and workspace sandboxing.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to generic OpenClaw exec auto-reviewer model selection.
- No changes to Codex app-server protocol fields or provider catalog configuration.

What does success look like?

- `lmstudio` / local provider Codex runs no longer send `approvalsReviewer: "auto_review"` or `guardian_subagent`.
- Existing provider-qualified legacy bindings such as `lmstudio/local-model` are handled safely even without a stored `modelProvider`.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The provider gate in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts`.
- The three request-shaping call sites in run attempts, conversation bindings, and side-question forks.
- Whether preserving `on-request` + sandbox while downgrading only the reviewer is the right compatibility boundary.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Maintainer-requested investigation and fix during local debugging.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: local/custom Codex harness model providers no longer use Codex Guardian / model-backed approval review for exec auto mode.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on the Codex plugin test harness using mocked Codex app-server request flows for `lmstudio` and provider-qualified local model refs.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`; `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: Vitest reported 4 passed files / 135 passed tests; oxfmt check passed; touched-file oxlint passed; `git diff --check` passed.
- Observed result after fix: mocked `lmstudio` Codex app-server start/turn, code-mode-only, side-question, and bound-conversation flows keep prompting approval and sandboxing but send `approvalsReviewer: "user"`; Codex/OpenAI providers still keep `auto_review`.
- What was not tested: a live LM Studio or Ollama app-server round trip with a real model process.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:changed` was attempted through Blacksmith Testbox, but both runs failed before checks started during remote dependency installation. First run hit pnpm's no-TTY module purge prompt (`tbx_01ksz13e4m3g69qp2pd4k0cr2w`); second run set `CI=true` in the remote command but failed during dependency postinstall for `@matrix-org/matrix-sdk-crypto-nodejs` / `esbuild` missing optional/postinstall dependencies (`tbx_01ksz17gpj3dwkn8k18k6rt257`).
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing config resolution forced `tools.exec.mode=auto` into Codex app-server `approvalsReviewer: "auto_review"` before considering the selected model provider.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Policy-level coverage for Codex/OpenAI preserving model-backed review and custom providers downgrading to user review.
- Run-attempt coverage for `lmstudio` + `tools.exec.mode=auto` request shaping.
- Bound-conversation coverage for stored custom `modelProvider` and legacy provider-qualified model refs.
- Native code-mode-only coverage for provider-qualified local Codex model refs on main turns and side-question forks.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The runtime policy was selected before model-provider scoping, so `tools.exec.mode=auto` could send model-backed Guardian review for non-OpenAI model providers.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Approval routing for Codex app-server exec auto mode when a run uses provider overrides or existing bound-session metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change narrows only model-backed approval review. It preserves prompting approval policy and sandbox posture for custom providers, and keeps Codex/OpenAI behavior unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Review the provider gate and request-shaping coverage, then run CI / changed checks once the remote dependency install issue is clear.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- `pnpm check:changed` remote proof is blocked by Testbox dependency installation before checks start.
- Live local-model app-server proof was not run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- None yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the code path and kept the change scoped to Codex app-server approval reviewer routing.
